Title: The glycoside hydrolase 28 member VdEPG1 is a virulence factor of Verticillium dahliae and interacts with the jasmonic acid pathway-related gene GhOPR9

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Verticillium dahliae; Gossypium hirsutum; Nicotiana benthamiana
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glycoside hydrolase family 28 (GH28); endopolygalacturonase; contains signal peptide
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Cell wall-derived sugar acid; oxylipin phytohormone
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Galacturonic acid derives from pectin degradation; jasmonic acid mediates plant defense against pathogen infection
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Plant cell wall/apoplast for pectin substrate context; cotton roots for JA context
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a virulence factor, hydrolyzes pectin to galacturonic acid, suppresses NLP1-triggered immune responses, and interferes with GhOPR9-mediated JA biosynthesis during infection
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Greatly induced in V. dahliae inoculated on cotton roots; highest-expressed GH28 member during root infection
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed during fungal infection of cotton roots; transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves in assays
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Secreted/extracellular; plasma membrane in plant-cell localization assays
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GhOPR9; functional suppression context with VdNLP1-induced cell death
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cotton root infection; osmotic stress (KCl, NaCl, sorbitol); different carbon sources; transient co-expression with NLP1
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene knockout and complementation in V. dahliae; pathogenicity assays on cotton; pectin hydrolysis LC-MS assay; yeast signal trap assay; transient expression and cell-death suppression assay; Y2H; BiFC; luciferase complementation imaging
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in fungal complementation; PVX vector for transient expression in N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VdEPG1 (VDAG_04977) was the GH28 member with strongest induction during cotton root infection. 2. Purified VdEPG1 hydrolyzed pectin to produce galacturonic acid. 3. VdEPG1 suppressed VdNLP1-induced cell death and reduced expression of several defense-related genes in N. benthamiana. 4. Deletion of VdEPG1 reduced virulence, conidiation, osmotic stress tolerance, carbon-source utilization, and cellophane penetration. 5. VdEPG1 physically interacted with cotton GhOPR9, linking fungal virulence to host JA biosynthesis-related defense.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion impaired fungal growth under osmotic stress, reduced conidiation, carbon utilization, and penetration ability
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for disease control by disrupting fungal virulence or blocking the VdEPG1–GhOPR9 interaction; useful for host-induced gene silencing or resistance breeding focused on preserving JA-mediated defense
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although VdEPG1 is an enzyme, its central role in this paper is as a fungal virulence factor interacting with host GhOPR9 rather than as a host nutrient-biosynthetic gene. It localizes to the plant plasma membrane in assays and may modulate host JA biosynthesis indirectly.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: OPR family, old yellow enzyme-like oxidoreductase
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: OPC-8:0 or reduced cyclopentenone intermediate leading to jasmonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces 12-oxo-phytodienoate-type jasmonate precursors in the jasmonic acid biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense against Verticillium dahliae and pathogen-responsive immunity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for biosynthesis of the target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes jasmonic acid accumulation and enhances resistance to Verticillium dahliae
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced in cotton roots after V. dahliae inoculation; reported previously to increase by more than 100-fold at 24 h after inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In cotton roots during early infection; transgenically overexpressed in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Virus-induced gene silencing in cotton; transgenic overexpression in Arabidopsis; JA quantification; disease index and fungal biomass assays; Y2H; BiFC; luciferase complementation imaging
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhOPR9 was identified as a VdEPG1-interacting cotton protein by Y2H and confirmed by BiFC and luciferase complementation. 2. Silencing GhOPR9 in cotton reduced JA accumulation and increased susceptibility to V. dahliae. 3. Overexpression of GhOPR9 in Arabidopsis enhanced resistance to V. dahliae. 4. GhOPR9 therefore positively contributes to JA biosynthesis-associated defense.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; described as part of the OPR2-dependent subsidiary JA biosynthesis pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful defense-related metabolic engineering target to enhance Verticillium resistance via boosting JA-associated immunity; deployment should consider pathway redundancy and crosstalk in JA signaling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper states GhOPR9 is a homolog of AtOPR2 and discusses that OPR2-dependent JA biosynthesis is subsidiary relative to OPR3-mediated JA biosynthesis. Silencing increased transcription of some other JA synthesis genes, suggesting compensatory feedback, but JA content still decreased.
